_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to calculate and interpret mean and standard deviation, and use them to compare data sets.

_source.w: We are learning to calculate and interpret mean and standard deviation, and use them to compare data sets.
y: Standard deviation quantifies how spread out data values are from the mean — it is the most widely used measure of variability in statistics.
Comparing datasets using both centre (mean) and spread (standard deviation) provides a more complete picture than either measure alone.
These statistics are used universally in research, quality control, finance, medicine and education.
eg: Calculating the standard deviation of a dataset and interpreting what a larger or smaller value means.
Comparing two classes: 'Both have a mean score of 75, but Class A has σ = 5 while Class B has σ = 15.'
Using the mean and standard deviation to identify unusual values (beyond 2 standard deviations).
Calculating these statistics using a calculator or spreadsheet.
Interpreting: 'A manufacturing process has mean length 50 mm and σ = 0.3 mm. Is a part at 51 mm acceptable?'
Comparing the variability of two investment options using standard deviation as a measure of risk.
